source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crane/Lifting Device

event.top_event: High pressure fluid release striking worker in face
event.incident_type: Loss of Containment - Pressurized Fluid Release
actions_taken: Worker assisted off BOP stack by Subsea Technician
Worker transported to medic on board vessel
Medivac called to transport worker to inland facility
event.summary: On June 9, 2015, a Subsea Engineer was struck in the face with high pressure BOP fluid while attempting to replace O-rings on a Sub Plate Mounted (SPM) valve on the BOP stack during stump testing operations. The worker was performing work on the shearable side SPM valve when the second bolt was being removed, releasing pressurized fluid that struck him directly in the face. The incident resulted in facial injuries and required medivac evacuation for treatment.

hazards.name: Poor ergonomic positioning of SPM valve

threats.name: Failure to depressurize BOP system before maintenance
Pressure gauge malfunction providing false zero reading
Isolation valves closed preventing pressure venting
Pressure buildup due to leaking PRV after line isolation
Worker positioned in line of fire during valve work

consequences.name: Facial injury from high pressure fluid impact
Operational downtime and work site shutdown

controls.name: Pressure gauge monitoring and verification
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to verify depressurization before maintenance

supporting_text: the pressure gauge for the Shearable SPM valve line was stuck at 0 psi, giving a false reading when checked by crew members
Photographs taken after the incident confirmed that the gauges were all reading 0 psi, although known pressure remained in the line

controls.name: Pressure Relief Valve maintenance and integrity
controls.barrier_role: Prevent pressure buildup in isolated lines
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineering control to manage system pressure

supporting_text: A leaking Pressure Relief Valve (PRV) in the system allowed pressure to build in the line leading to the SPM valve

controls.name: Line depressurization and venting procedure
controls.barrier_role: Ensure complete pressure relief before maintenance work
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to prevent pressurized fluid release

supporting_text: Although the crew members bleed the lines of pressure, the isolation valves were placed back into the closed position afterwards, preventing a vent path, and allowing pressure to build in the lines due to the leaking PRV
The Subsea Engineer proceeded to work on the SPM valve without first ensuring that all pressure had been removed from the lines

controls.name: Risk assessment for maintenance tasks
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and controls for all work activities
controls.lod_basis: Management control to ensure hazard identification before work

supporting_text: Although a Risk Assessment had been performed by the crew before the work began for the day, the task of replacing the O-rings on the SPM valves was not addressed in the work scope
Failure of the crew members to preform a proper risk assessment after adding the task of changing out the SPM valve O-rings was added to the plan. The job was thought to be 'routine'

controls.name: Personal Protective Equipment - face/eye protection
controls.barrier_role: Protect worker from fluid impact to face and eyes
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense to minimize injury severity

supporting_text: The pressurized fluid struck the IP directly in the face, protected only by his safety glasses

controls.name: Work positioning and ergonomic assessment
controls.barrier_role: Prevent worker from being positioned in line of fire
controls.lod_basis: Control to eliminate hazardous work positioning

supporting_text: The SPM valve posed a risk in that it was in a poor location. The placement of the valve put whoever was attempting to work on it in a confined area with little room to move, forcing them into the line of fire
Placement of the SPM Valve puts employees in an awkward position, bad body placement, when attempting to perform work on the valve

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
